bodyHTML:

Arc Flash Study

CareLabs is a low-cost provider of electrical arc flash analysis, investigation, and certification services for all sorts of enterprises. Arc flash hazards, staff training, documentation, and PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) needs will all be evaluated by qualified professionals to ensure that your electrical safety program is current, compliant, and complete. CareLabs can detect arc flash concerns early on and take suitable actions to limit the impact of harmonic distortions in order to assure the equipment’s safety. To analyze and evaluate Arc flashes, ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program) software is used. 

Enquire
Contact Us

An arc flash is the amount of light and heat produced by an arc fault. This happens when current travels via an air gap between two conductors. The arc flash ionizes the air and produces a significant amount of light and heat (approximately 35,000 degrees Fahrenheit). Arc flash analysis is the process of evaluating and analysing a facility’s electrical systems to determine the incident energy available for each piece of electrical equipment. Arc flash studies promote safe working conditions by developing safety requirements for skilled electricians working on equipment and circuits. Revised electrical one-line schematics and equipment locations are also included, as well as a short circuit and coordination analysis. 

Objectives of Arc Flash Research:
  • Determine the magnitude of the damage caused by incident energy radiated during an arc flash fault.
  • Adjusting protective equipment settings to limit exposure to incident energy
  • Make safety advice for arc flash hazards.
  • Follow your country’s unique regulations and legislation.
  • Determine the sort of PPE that will be worn at work.
The Negative Effects of Arc Flash:
  • Arcs cause serious burns to the skin, eyes, and face.
  • Inhaling gases and hot particles can result in serious burns to the throat and lungs.
  • Injuries are caused by flying debris and loose pieces.

Arc flash studies are critical for reducing hazards and improving the safety of electrical workers, as well as the overall safety of the organization. Arc flash studies assist in assessing electrical danger levels and putting in place the necessary safety precautions to lower the risk of burns and accidents. Arc flash analysis is required for all utilities, manufacturing, industrial, and commercial organizations that employ electrical disconnects, motor control, panel boards, and switchboards. 

Arc Flash Research Advantages:
  • Improved mechanical and human safety
  • Reduced medical and legal costs
  • Observance of regulatory safety requirements
  • Reduced norm and penalty violations

The following steps are used for arc flash analysis: 

  • Data gathering
  • Inspection in the field
  • Design of a system
  • Arc flash examination
Arc Flash Research: CareLabs Work Method

CareLabs provides outstanding services and will provide any services needed by the construction team, local contractor, or engineering firm.

Our work process consists of the following steps:

  • Data gathering
  • ETAP simulation of a power system
  • Short-circuit testing
  • Overcurrent protection device coordination
  • An inquiry into arc flashes
  • Check for arc flashes.
  • Maintaining a ledger
  • Methods for reducing the likelihood of an arc flash
  • One-line diagrams in full size
  • Arc flash marking installation
  • The completed report
Advantages of Using CareLab Services:
  • Create more secure electrical systems that are compliant with rules.
  • Protective devices and bus ratings are paired with integrated modules for thorough short circuit, overcurrent coordination, device assessment, and arc flash evaluation.
  • Making the workplace more secure
  • Simple alternative design recommendation
  • Increased safety margins with the usage of user-defined arc fault tolerances
  • Our computer-aided analysis will save you time by automatically developing arc flash labels.
  • Reduce lost productivity and maintenance expenditures.

Crew members are strategically placed across CareLabs to guarantee that our specialists are nearby in the event of a routine or emergency scenario. CareLabs has quickly established itself as an ISO 9001:2008-accredited company with a satisfied customer base. We provide arc flash investigation and analysis services in all major cities, including Helsinki, Vantaa, Espoo, and Tampere. Please contact us right away for a free quote on arc flash study and analysis.

title: Harmonic Study and Analysis
bodyHTML:
• Home
  • About Us
  • Our Services

    • Harmonic Study and Analysis

    • Motor Start Analysis
  • Contact Us

Harmonic Study and Analysis

CareLabs provides electrical installation services such as electrical safety inspections, testing, calibration, and certification, as well as a full harmonic investigation and analysis. Professionals will examine harmonic hazards, training programs, documentation, and personal protective equipment (PPE) to ensure that your electrical safety program is current, compliant, and comprehensive.

Enquire
Contact Us

CareLabs can discover harmonics early on and take the required actions to decrease their effect, ensuring the safety of the equipment. In an electrical system, harmonic electric voltages and currents can cause inefficiencies. They generate inefficient heat and can degrade electrical quality. Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical approach for predicting potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels. Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical approach that uses empirical evidence to anticipate probable resonances and harmonic distortion levels.

Nonlinear electrical switching equipment such as AC variable frequency motors, DC drives, fluorescent lights, and computers include harmonic frequencies into the electrical system. High harmonic voltage/current levels pose a serious risk of equipment failure. As a result, a timely harmonic analysis and study is critical. Harmonic restrictions for 415-volt systems are now 5%, with 4% odd harmonics and 2% even harmonics. 

Harmonic Distortion Effects:
  • In charge of high voltage and circulating current.
  • Failure and destruction of equipment as a result of significant voltage distortion
  • An increase in component internal energy, resulting in component failure and a decreased lifespan.
  • Branch circuit breaker tripping due to a fault
  • Error in measurement
  • Explosions and fires in electrical and distribution systems
  • Generator breakdowns
  • A decrease in system power factor
  • The installation and utility expenditures are high.
  • Productivity declines
  • Inactivity and unnecessary driving
  • The use of larger motors

Harmonic difficulties are extremely infrequent, but when they do occur, they tend to reduce the reliability, power quality, and efficiency of the power system. As a result, harmonic study and analysis are critical. It safeguards against current and voltage harmonics. Frequency variance limitation 

  • Current and voltage imbalances
  • Improve power factor analysis and keep the value near to one.
  • Voltage fluctuations are regulated.

Harmonic research and analysis will provide insight into the source and effect of power quality issues, assisting in the avoidance of harmonic-related concerns during the design phase and reducing the risk of unexpected consequences. 

Workflow for Harmonic Study and Analysis in CareLabs:
  • On the electrical single-line diagram, highlight available nonlinear loads, capacitor banks, and long medium-voltage cables.
  • Find the common connection point (PCC (Point of Common Coupling))
  • Determine which system buses inside the facility are susceptible to harmonic distortions.
  • Collect harmonics data for all nonlinear loads.
  • Obtain critical information about voltage and current harmonics at the chosen PCC, such as the maximum and minimum short circuit fault levels and Australia’s permissible harmonic values.
  • Using ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program), do a harmonic analysis of the electrical network.
  • Calculate the total and individual values of current and voltage wave distortion at the buses and PCC. Create a harmonic frequency spectrum.
  • When harmonic distortion levels exceed permissible limits, an appropriate mitigation approach is proposed and implemented.
  • Rerun the analysis after installing the mitigation solution.
CareLabs Service Advantages:

If background harmonics have been measured before, the second stage consists of higher ratings.

  • If the background is less than 25%, Stage 2 allows for adjusting converter size based on fault level and converter type.
  • Half of the upper converter grade is allowed if the background is between 25% and 75%.
  • A comprehensive harmonic analysis is performed for the upper background.

CareLabs has crew members stationed around the country to guarantee that our specialists are nearby in the event of a routine or emergency crisis. CareLabs has quickly established itself as an ISO 9001:2008-accredited firm with a dedicated customer base that has provided excellent feedback. We do harmonic research and analysis in Helsinki, Vantaa, Espoo, and Tampere, among other significant Finland cities. Please contact us right away to organize your harmonic analysis and study in Finnish or to obtain a quote!

title: Motor Start Analysis
bodyHTML:
• Home
  • About Us
  • Our Services
    • Arc Flash Study
    • Harmonic Study and Analysis
    • Power Quality Analysis
    • Power System Study and Analysis
    • Motor Start Analysis
  • Contact Us

Motor Start Analysis

Businesses in Finnish may use CareLabs’ Motor start research and analysis service. Our motor start analysis service’s primary goal is to discover what unusual events occur when a big motor is started. Other variables to check before starting an electric motor include ensuring that the electric motor is appropriately linked to the line voltage.

Enquire
Contact Us

Nuisance tripping, excessive nominal currents, and flickering or fading lights, for example, are all signs of a faulty electrical system. CareLabs will send qualified personnel to your location to gather current and historical data on the installation of your motors. Then, using the manufacturer’s data and information, calculations are made to predict what will happen in the future and design strategies to prevent the probable repercussions.

Methods for starting a motor can be investigated using both static and dynamic modelling. We are particularly interested in dynamic motor starting research, which attempts to mimic motor starting circumstances such as:

  • The electric motor’s measured starting impedance is used to model its load flow solution.
  • The short-circuit approach is used to calculate current under pre-fault short circuit conditions, whereas the voltage drop method is used to calculate current in a motor when it is first turned on.
  • When an accurate dynamic model of the electric motor circuit and load-torque characteristics is available, the dynamic model of the electric motor may be used to assess the influence of the motor starting.
  • Transient stability simulations may be used when a thorough dynamic model of the electric motor is not available.
Finland Should Do a Motor Start Analysis:

Motors are employed in modern industrial systems, and the size of electric motors is increasing at an exponential rate. If you turn on a huge motor that is connected to the line, it might cause serious problems for the electric motor, any neighboring loads, and distant buses. To start an electric motor, the terminal voltage must be at least 80% of the rated voltage or higher. Because the square of a motor’s terminal voltage is proportional to its load torque, voltage difference has an instantaneous effect on the motor’s load torque characteristic. 

If the voltage drops enough, the pressure on the functioning machines may be greater than their “breakdown torque,” forcing them to slow down or perhaps stop. The voltage drop also affects other sorts of loads, such as electrical equipment, sensitive control devices, lighting loads, and so on. Furthermore, high starting current lowers terminal voltage, leading all systems that serve a constant load to use additional current. Furthermore, low-voltage relays have been shown to be faulty. 

When Is It Necessary to Do a Motor Start Analysis?
  • Before or during the purchase of a huge engine.
  • If no generator is present and the motor rating is greater than 30% of the supply transformer’s base KVA rating.
  • The generator should be replaced if it is the only source of power for the system and the motor rating reaches 10-15% of the generator’s KVA rating.
  • A motor start analysis may be necessary if numerous motors start at the same time for a process in an industry.
  • Because smaller power systems often have less power, voltage drop difficulties are compounded when starting a high-power motor. Examining motor beginning may help to better understand the output of small systems with generators.
Benefits of Motor Start Evaluation:

Analyses of how a motor gets started Select a motor or starting technique that has the best speed-torque characteristics for the job. Specific loads must be accelerated under close supervision to prevent exceeding the torque restrictions of the equipment. The shaft of the motor can be checked at any time during a motor start investigation. 

As a result, it assists in evaluating how much torque the equipment can withstand. Motor starting study is needed to investigate how starting affects machine voltage and if an electric motor can start and accelerate efficiently.

CareLabs offers motor start tests performed by experienced technicians to help you analyze the operation of your electric motor. We provide motor start analysis services in all major cities, including Helsinki, Vantaa, Espoo, and Tampere. Please contact us as soon as possible to schedule your motor starting study or to obtain a quote.

title: Power Quality Analysis
bodyHTML:
• Home
  • About Us
  • Our Services
    • Arc Flash Study
    • Harmonic Study and Analysis
    • Power Quality Analysis
    • Power System Study and Analysis
    • Motor Start Analysis
  • Contact Us

Power Quality Analysis

CareLabs examines the power quality of Finland businesses. Our goal is to protect your electrical installations, electrical equipment, and appliances against power supply variations

Enquire
Contact Us

Many businesses and an increasing number of individual customers today rely on data collection, monitoring, and instrumentation equipment that is very sensitive to power supply fluctuations. Few electrical equipment are designed to withstand the voltage fluctuation spectrum that is a common feature of distributed electricity. CareLabs can help you with power quality issues associated with equipment installation, control, protection, and power conditioning. 

Power Quality Analysis Objective:
  • Disagreements in the electrical system and load are evaluated.
  • Evaluating the workplace to improve simulation methods for offering power quality solutions.
  • Predicting load equipment efficiency and operation
  • Improve the power factor and reliability of the machine.
  • Avoid using too much energy.
  • To avoid faults and disruptions.
  • Reducing power quality anomalies such as harmonics, spikes, and transients.
  • Assist in preventing voltage/frequency spikes and PLC lockups.
  • To avoid overheated transformers, capacitor explosions, and nuisance tripping.
  • Reduce your utility expenditure dramatically.
  • Extend the service life of the network and its components.
  • Inspect electrical equipment to ensure that it can withstand modifications.
Power Quality Analysis is Required in Finland:

All electrical networks have supply outages, which become more common when storms, natural disasters, or accidents destroy power lines.

Power supply fluctuations are very normal. In the qualities of power transmitted by an electricity network, there are three categories of fluctuations, disturbances, and distortions.

1.A frequency deviates from its typical frequency range when it exceeds it.

2. Voltage disruptions occur when the voltage form is maintained but the voltage amplitude exceeds the normal range.

2.1 Voltage drops (dips) and voltage spikes of up to one minute duration are examples of short-duration variations.

2.2 Long duration voltage fluctuations are under voltages and over voltages that last more than one minute.

2.3 Unbalanced voltage occurs when the load is distributed unevenly across the phases, causing significant equipment heating.

2.4 Voltage differences between neutral and ground

3.Disturbances caused by voltage distortion – when the voltage is not uniform or sinusoidal in shape.

3.1 Device heating, increased losses, inefficient power utilization, and resonance issues are all caused by harmonics and inter harmonics.

3.2 Transients lasting less than a tenth of a second and capable of damaging switch contact surfaces, creating system inefficiencies, and causing motor drives and control to fail prematurely.

What is Included in a Power Quality Analysis?

Identifying various power quality concerns involves the use of various methodologies, such as the following:

  • Harmonic Analysis is the use of computer algorithms to detect and predict potential harmonic problems, as well as reduction methods.
  • Surge and Transient Analysis: Surges and transients will be identified in this inquiry.
  • This study would track the voltage’s short-term drop and rise, as well as its value and trajectory.
  • Reactive Force Evaluation The preferred reactive power at the distribution and load ends will be determined by this investigation.
  • This project would measure and synchronize captive electricity in order to meet demand while lowering energy surcharges and fuel use.
  • Depending on the operating circumstances, this analysis predicts power flow magnitudes, power factor, voltage levels, and losses in system branches.

CareLabs provides the best service possible in order to improve the quality and dependability of your electrical installation for the benefit of all of its clients. We provide power quality analysis in Helsinki, Vantaa, Espoo, and Tampere, among other significant cities. Contact us right now to schedule an inspection of your electrical quality or to acquire a quote!

bodyText:
Home
About Us
Our Services
Contact Us
Power Quality Analysis

CareLabs examines the power quality of Finland businesses. Our goal is to protect your electrical installations, electrical equipment, and appliances against power supply variations

Enquire
Contact Us

Many businesses and an increasing number of individual customers today rely on data collection, monitoring, and instrumentation equipment that is very sensitive to power supply fluctuations. Few electrical equipment are designed to withstand the voltage fluctuation spectrum that is a common feature of distributed electricity. CareLabs can help you with power quality issues associated with equipment installation, control, protection, and power conditioning. 

Power Quality Analysis Objective:
Disagreements in the electrical system and load are evaluated.
Evaluating the workplace to improve simulation methods for offering power quality solutions.
Predicting load equipment efficiency and operation
Improve the power factor and reliability of the machine.
Avoid using too much energy.
To avoid faults and disruptions.
Reducing power quality anomalies such as harmonics, spikes, and transients.
Assist in preventing voltage/frequency spikes and PLC lockups.
To avoid overheated transformers, capacitor explosions, and nuisance tripping.
Reduce your utility expenditure dramatically.
Extend the service life of the network and its components.
Inspect electrical equipment to ensure that it can withstand modifications.
Power Quality Analysis is Required in Finland:

All electrical networks have supply outages, which become more common when storms, natural disasters, or accidents destroy power lines.

Power supply fluctuations are very normal. In the qualities of power transmitted by an electricity network, there are three categories of fluctuations, disturbances, and distortions.

1.A frequency deviates from its typical frequency range when it exceeds it.

2. Voltage disruptions occur when the voltage form is maintained but the voltage amplitude exceeds the normal range.

2.1 Voltage drops (dips) and voltage spikes of up to one minute duration are examples of short-duration variations.

2.2 Long duration voltage fluctuations are under voltages and over voltages that last more than one minute.

2.3 Unbalanced voltage occurs when the load is distributed unevenly across the phases, causing significant equipment heating.

2.4 Voltage differences between neutral and ground

3.Disturbances caused by voltage distortion – when the voltage is not uniform or sinusoidal in shape.

3.1 Device heating, increased losses, inefficient power utilization, and resonance issues are all caused by harmonics and inter harmonics.

3.2 Transients lasting less than a tenth of a second and capable of damaging switch contact surfaces, creating system inefficiencies, and causing motor drives and control to fail prematurely.

What is Included in a Power Quality Analysis?

Identifying various power quality concerns involves the use of various methodologies, such as the following:

Harmonic Analysis is the use of computer algorithms to detect and predict potential harmonic problems, as well as reduction methods.
Surge and Transient Analysis: Surges and transients will be identified in this inquiry.
This study would track the voltage’s short-term drop and rise, as well as its value and trajectory.
Reactive Force Evaluation The preferred reactive power at the distribution and load ends will be determined by this investigation.
This project would measure and synchronize captive electricity in order to meet demand while lowering energy surcharges and fuel use.
Depending on the operating circumstances, this analysis predicts power flow magnitudes, power factor, voltage levels, and losses in system branches.

CareLabs provides the best service possible in order to improve the quality and dependability of your electrical installation for the benefit of all of its clients. We provide power quality analysis in Helsinki, Vantaa, Espoo, and Tampere, among other significant cities. Contact us right now to schedule an inspection of your electrical quality or to acquire a quote!

title: Power System Study and Analysis
bodyHTML:
• Home
  • About Us
  • Our Services
    • Arc Flash Study
    • Harmonic Study and Analysis
    • Power Quality Analysis
    • Power System Study and Analysis
    • Motor Start Analysis
  • Contact Us

Power System Study and Analysis

CareLabs offers a variety of electrical installation services, including electrical safety inspections, simulations of electrical systems, power quality analysis studies, and power system research and analysis.

Enquire
Contact Us

ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program), the most effective tool for power system transient analysis, is used by our team of electrical specialists to conduct load flow and short circuit studies, protective device coordination studies, transient analysis, network stability investigations, and harmonic emission and filter design research. It allows us to collaborate directly with clients to design and implement the most effective power compensation and solutions. 

Why is a Power System Analysis Required?

Every industrial or commercial facility needs a reliable, dependable, and well-maintained electrical system. Even a little power outage or failure can have disastrous consequences, including extended downtime, damage to critical equipment, and the expense of restoring it. Periodic power system analysis may confirm that your facility’s power system is safe, efficient, and dependable in both normal and abnormal circumstances. 

A power system must be thoroughly analyzed at each step of design, installation, and operation. Our power system studies and evaluations will provide an overview of how the system will operate under normal operation and transient fault conditions using the ETAP software tool. It refers to a collection of various engineering analytical studies used to investigate the response of an electric power system to events across time.

Several investigations have revealed the state of your facility’s electricity system. A power system analysis study report may include the following examples:

  • The circuit breaker
  • Procedures for safety coordination
  • Load Transport
  • Dynamic and transient evaluation
  • Examination of Arc Flashes
  • Power Efficiency
  • Harmonic analysis
  • Start the engine.
  • Earthing research

It assists businesses in developing a standard for other aspects of overall plant performance, such as the electrical safety program. These findings suggest that safe and dependable operations are possible. 

Load Flow Analysis:

Load flow studies compute voltages and currents in various parts of the network, as well as the selection of equipment with the appropriate rating. ETAP’s built-in load flow function may be used to perform load flows, which provide information on the power loading, power factor, voltages, and currents of a power system. 

Investigations into Short-Circuits:

Short-circuit investigations on a power system are required to determine how fault situations emerge. Manually performing short circuit studies is a time-consuming endeavor, and competent software such as ETAP is significantly more efficient. ETAP is used to simulate system protection devices and evaluate the impact of three-phase, L-G, L-L, and L-L-G distribution system failures. 

Transient Investigation:

Transient studies evaluate the amount and types of transient overvoltage and currents caused by lightning strikes and switching processes. The results define the amount of insulation and surge protection required. 

Protection Coordination Research:

This study is intended to validate your system’s various security devices. It ensures that they are properly coordinated and scaled for the item they are protecting.

Our power system analysis approach frequently incorporates the following critical functions:

  • Method of data collection
  • Data analysis and client meetings
  • Gathering equipment data
  • A single-line diagram model
  • ETAP power system simulation
  • Short circuit and load flow analysis
  • Protection collaboration investigations
  • Arc flash analysis
  • A synopsis of the study report.
  • Planning for risk minimization and risk assessment
  • Project management
The Carelabs Power System Analysis Is Made Up of the Following Components:
  • Options for lowering energy prices and use.
  • Reports that highlight problem areas.
  • Solutions suggestions
  • Information on how and where energy is used.
  • Ratings of safety devices must be verified.
  • Aid in the prevention of harm and injury.
  • Harmonics and transients’ effects

Our power system analysis studies are focused on analysing the client’s electrical system and suggesting improvements to its performance and supply security. We provide power system analysis and study services in Helsinki, Vantaa, Espoo, and Tampere, among other places. Please contact us as soon as possible to organize a study and analysis of your power system or to acquire a quote!
